rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DICK VS. LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASSOCIATION Notice Of And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Of Plaintiff is continued to June 23, 2023 for opposing party to comply with SFRL 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of opposition no later than May 31, 2023 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date (moving papers and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BUSH STREET APARTMENT GROUP, LLC. VS. BALMORAL RESIDENCES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT YALE RESIDENCES, LLC, TONY BRETTKELLY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as requested by the moving defendants Yale Residences and Tony Brettkelly (alter ego) on the Second Cause of Action (Breach of Written Contract), the Sixth Cause of Action (Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing), and the Seventh Cause of Action (Negligence) as to the claim for monthly rent damages from August 2020 through June 2026 . Moving parties shifter their burden to establish that the corporate tenant is a tier 1 commercial tenant entitled to protections of the SF Administrative Code 37C and that there is no liability for rent by the individual defendant, based only on alter ego (motion for summary judgment is framed by the pleadings). Opposing party failed to raise any triable issues of fact. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO MENDOZA LUNA VS. BEHROUZ HALALI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 25, 2023 line 6. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION Line 6: Luna v. Halali, 605849 (Judge Feng) OSC: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. Defendants and all persons actin on their behalf are restrained from doing, directly or indirectly the following acts pending the resolution of this action or further order of this Court: (1) blocking Plaintiff's (and family) access to any common areas unless access is interrupted temporarily for a lawful purpose (e.g. for repairs), for a reasonable amount of time, and with a reasonable notice; (2) changing the locks to Plaintiff's unit or common areas unless the key is immediately provided to the Plaintiff if the locks are changed when Plaintiff is at the premises or Defendants make the keys available to the Plaintiff for pick up immediately upon Plaintiff's return to the premises (e.g. Plaintiff's return from work, vacation, etc.); (3) terminating Plaintiff's housing services, including, but not limited to utilities, cable, internet, etc. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
